rag_rules: Place input envelope assumptions in component type annexes using visible port names and explicit numeric bounds from the requirement.
Use eq to define boolean range predicates when expressing numeric constraints, ensuring type alignment with AADL port types.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees must reference only visible, explicitly declared features or constants from the AADL model.
Numeric bounds in AGREE clauses must be directly stated in the requirement—do not infer or generalize ranges.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use annex agree {** ... **}; for component type contracts; avoid assign in type scope.
Define range conditions as boolean equations: eq range_check : bool = port >= min and port <= max;
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not convert Boolean or opaque ports into numeric comparisons unless explicitly typed as real/int in AADL.
